title: Philosophy
content:
I.

В В В В His eyes found nothing beautiful and bright,
В В В В Nor wealth nor, honour, glory nor delight,
В В В В Which he could grasp and keep with might and right.

В В В В Flowers bloomed for maidens, swords outflashed for boys,
В В В В The worldпїЅs big children had their various toys;
В В В В He could not feel their sorrows and their joys.

В В В В Hills held a secret they would not unfold,
В В В В In careless scorn of him the ocean rolled,
В В В В The stars were alien splendours high and cold.

В В В В He felt himself a king bereft of crown,
В В В В Defrauded from his birthright of renown,
В В В В Bred up in littleness with churl and clown.



II.

В В В В How could he vindicate himself? His eyes,
В В В В That found not anywhere their proper prize,
В В В В Looked through and through the specious earth and skies,

В В В В They probed, and all things yielded to their probe;
В В В В They saw the void around the massy globe,
В В В В The raging fire within its flowery robe.

В В В В They pierced through beauty; saw the bones, the mesh
В В В В Of nerves and veins, the hideous raw red flesh,
В В В В Beneath the skin most delicate and fresh:

В В В В Saw Space a mist unfurled around the steep
В В В В Where plunge TimeпїЅs waters to the blackest deep;
В В В В Saw Life a dream in DeathпїЅs eternal sleep.



III.

В В В В A certain fair form came before his sight,
В В В В Responding to him as the day to night
В В В В To yearning, love; to cold and gloom, warm light.

В В В В A hope sprang from his breast, and fluttered far
В В В В On rainbow wings; beyond the cloudy bar,
В В В В Though very much beneath the nearest star.

В В В В His eyes drew back their beams to kindle fire
В В В В In his own heart; whose masterful desire
В В В В Scorned all beyond its aim, lower or higher.

В В В В This fire flung lustre upon grace and bloom,
В В В В Gave warmth and brightness to a little room,
В В В В Burned Thought to ashes in its fight with gloom.



IV.

В В В В He said: Those eyes alone see well that view
В В В В LifeпїЅs lovely surfaces of form and hue;
В В В В And not DeathпїЅs entrails, looking through and through.

В В В В Bones, nerves, and veins, and flesh, are covered in
В В В В By this opaque transparency of skin,
В В В В Precisely that we should not see within.

В В В В The corpse is hid, that Death may work its vile
В В В В Corruption in black secrecy; the while
В В В В Our saddest graves with grass and fair flowers smile.

В В В В If you will analyse the bread you eat,
В В В В The water and the wine most pure and sweet,
В В В В Your stomach soon must loathe all drink and meat.

В В В В Life liveth but in Life, and doth not roam
В В В В To other realms if all be well at home:
В В В В пїЅSolid as ocean-foam,пїЅ quoth ocean-foam.

В В В В If Midge will pine and curse its hours away
В В В В Because Midge is not Everything For-aye,
В В В В Poor Midge thus loses its one summer day;
В В В В Loses its all, and winneth what, I pray?
meaning: About: James Thomson (November 23, 1834 - June 3, 1882), published under the pseudonym Bysshe Vanolis, was a Victorian-era British poet famous primarily for the long poem The City of Dreadful Night (1874), an expression of bleak pessimism in a dehumanized, uncaring urban environment.
